title: polypodium macaronesicum subsp. azoricum
intro: Polypodium macaronesicum subsp. azoricum is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Polypodium macaronesicum fern, native to the Azores Islands in Portugal. It is a type of vascular plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and even have antiviral and antioxidant effects. Traditionally, Polypodium macaronesicum subsp. azoricum has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments to support the treatment of various health conditions, although modern research on its efficacy is limited.
